title: preonanthus scherfelii
intro_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Preonanthus scherfelii, being a member of the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae), may have benefits in treating external skin sores and possibly even influencing the healing process due to the presence of the compound protoanemonin.
side_effects: Preonanthus scherfelii can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and stomach pain. In severe cases, it may also cause allergic reactions, such as hives and difficulty breathing. Additionally, overconsumption of the plant may lead to interactions with other medications, resulting in adverse effects on the cardiovascular system. It is essential to consult a medical professional before using Preonanthus scherfelii to avoid these potential complications.
